probeMetadata.bugUnderInvestigation: Race condition: switching between shopping lists sometimes shows stale items from previous list
testApproach: Created automated Playwright tests to probe list switching behavior
Tested normal list switching with 1000ms wait between switches
Tested rapid list switching (30 attempts) with no wait between clicks
Tested with simulated 200ms network latency (20 attempts)
Verified item content correctness after rapid switching

observations.baselineSwitching: PASS - headings correctly change between lists
observations.latencyTestStaleStates: 0/20 - no stale states even with network delay
observations.itemContentCorrect: PASS - list items correctly update on switch
observations.loadingStatesVisible: NO - instant transitions, no loading indicator

codeAnalysis.potentialRaceCondition: The useEffect cleanup may not complete before new subscription starts, but React's effect cleanup ordering should prevent this
mitigatingFactors: React's useEffect cleanup runs synchronously before the next effect
Initial fetch is scoped to listId, so even if old callback fires, it won't override new list's data

probeMetadata.conclusion: The reported race condition was NOT reproducible in automated testing. The subscription cleanup/setup appears to be working correctly. The bug may be:
possibleExplanations: Intermittent and requires specific timing/network conditions not simulated
Related to Supabase Realtime events arriving at specific moments
User perception issue rather than actual data inconsistency
Already fixed in a recent code change
Only occurs on slower devices or with specific browser conditions
recommendations: Add user-visible loading state during list transitions to improve perceived reliability
Consider adding a subscription ID check in the callback to ignore stale responses
Request more details from users experiencing the issue (browser, network, specific steps)
